questions.question: A cylindrical tank has a radius of 4 cm and a height of 15 cm. What is the volume of the tank in cubic centimeters? (Use π ≈ 3.14)
questions.explanation: Volume of a cylinder = πr²h. V = 3.14 × 4² × 15 = 3.14 × 16 × 15 = 753.6 cm³.

questions.question: A cone has a base radius of 6 inches and a height of 10 inches. A sphere has a radius of 6 inches. Which solid has greater volume, and by how much? (Use π ≈ 3.14; round to nearest whole number)
questions.explanation: Cone: V = (1/3)πr²h = (1/3) × 3.14 × 36 × 10 = 376.8 in³. Sphere: V = (4/3)πr³ = (4/3) × 3.14 × 216 = 904.32 in³. Difference: 904.32 − 376.8 ≈ 527.5, but recalculating: sphere is 527.5 in³ more.

questions.question: A rectangular prism has dimensions 5 cm × 8 cm × 12 cm. What is its surface area in square centimeters?
A cylinder has a volume of 450π cubic meters and a height of 18 meters. What is the radius of the cylinder?
questions.explanation: Volume = πr²h, so 450π = πr² × 18. Divide by π: 450 = 18r². Then r² = 25, so r = 5 meters.

questions.question: A sphere has a surface area of 100π square feet. What is its radius?
questions.explanation: Surface area of a sphere = 4πr². So 100π = 4πr². Divide by 4π: r² = 25, thus r = 5 feet.

questions.question: A cone and a cylinder share the same base radius of 3 cm and the same height of 9 cm. If the cone's volume is V_c and the cylinder's volume is V_cy, what is the ratio V_c : V_cy?
questions.explanation: Cone volume = (1/3)πr²h and cylinder volume = πr²h. The ratio is [(1/3)πr²h] / [πr²h] = 1/3, or 1:3.
